note: My Wedding ❤️  Wowww where do I even begin  We woke up and I made breakfast and transcribed my vows into the vow book. It was fun to hang out more with Jason and visit. We got all ready and left. As we approached Brigham I realized that I had forgotten my shoes agaaaain. I was so upset and kind of had a hard time letting it go. We picked up another pair in the Brigham Walmart.   We got to the venue around 3pm and greeted the few people that were already there. It was fun feeling their excitement and getting excited myself. I was really worried about Regina being too demanding of Maranda but that ended up being ok. We got the few things we had set up. Everyone had such anxious energy. I think they wanted to be helping but there really wasn’t anything to do. Logan had forgotten his shoes and Karlin was having a hard time letting it go, I shared my shoe story with them and reminded them that having a good time was more important than things being perfect. I gave them my groom stamp of approval. The time drew closer to the big moment. I was feeling so nervous. I got dressed and ready and asked everyone to clear out of the groom suite except for Brennan. It was so amazing to have him there. I realized quickly that my freshly bought shoes had white soles and that I felt a little ridiculous in them. Brennan offered his shoes up and I gladly took him up on it. It meant a lot to me and was excellent best man behavior.  The time finally came and the bridal party lined up. DJ Pauly was doing crowd hype which was amazing and a little worrisome, Nastasia literally said involuntarily, what is he doing??! I was like ooooo dramaaaa. Haha.   I escorted Mom down the aisle and we both embraced and cried together. Then I took my position up on the stage with Karen. I was feeling like the music was a little too loud. But it was all lovely. I looked out over the crowd and saw everyone in my life most important to me. It was special. I wish I had a picture of it. Rows and rows of my friends family and coworkers that made my life rich and made me feel special, all smiling at me and anxiously waiting for beautiful Maranda to come down the aisle. She timidly came out and began walking with her dads down towards us. As she came out invisible string began playing which made me cry so hard. She looks stunningly beautiful. She was also crying. Her dads hugged her and sat down. Doug had a particularly calm and peaceful look on his face which was lovely.  The ceremony began, Karen welcomed everyone and gave an amazing speech about love and connection. She told about her experience getting to know Maranda, and shared details of what made me and Maranda so special to each other. It was really sweet. We then decided to share vows and Maranda began hers with sharing the binoculars heart boner story. It was so cute. She talked about me being her home, she talked about how excited she was to have children with me. And that my kids will be lucky to have me as a dad. I then shared mine which I confidently read. They crushed 🤷🏻‍♂️ I meant every word.  The time came to share the rings. We did our little look under your chair bit and Hannah brought the ring box up to us. She looked at Maranda’s with tears and said thank you. 🥲 it was a lovely moment.  We said I do and were pronounced the McDuffins. Everyone cheered. We went back to the brides room and got to have a few minutes to ourselves. Regina barged in accidentally looking for her vape, I semi politely told her hurry get it and get out. She backed off and we got time back to ourselves.  We went back out for pictures which started with my family. We got them done and took a few friend photos.  After pics they got dinner set up and we ate our delicious meal. The salmon was a highlight for me. After a while I went down and joined some tables and got to visit with my coworkers and me and Mark took shots back and forth. It was fun. I then went to the Matt and Hayley table and got to hang with them. It was great to see everyone.  I grabbed a second plate and got halfway through it at which point I let Selene finish it because she wasn’t able to get seconds. We did the toasts next and Brennan went first. It was fun watching his face light up to mulberry street playing. His speech was heartfelt funny and sweet. He said I was his first best friend and that meant so much. Carley went next and did a really good job. She made Maranda cry. It was very sweet. Karlin came up next and told everyone about all of our adventures. She was eloquent and funny.  After the toasts we finished dinner up and started the second round of pictures. At this point I started feeling a little anxious because I could see that things might be getting kind of stale and awkward. We got through pictures and prepared for our grand entrance. I was feeling really anxious to get back out there.   We did our grand entrance and were announced which was kind of weird because dj Pauly played the movie fanfare song which was a bit much. We then surfed from group to group thanking everyone for coming. It was exhausting and I was really worried about not getting to everyone. Eric and Chelsea made it! It was really good to see them. I miss Eric. I learned later that Bryar was pretty affected by seeing him. I felt bad. Caitrin came and it was good to see her. I wished that I had invited her to join the dance party later on. All of my aunts made it and it was fun to see them and talk with them. Jon Jenny and Ryan made it with his baby. It was good to see them. They hung out mostly with Devan. We saw Marie and Maranda was obsessed with her haircut. We saw Emily, it was good to see her. Jenna and Trevor Hulse showed up! Fun to talk to them.   Dj Pauly then announced the cake cutting. It was very cute and I didn’t even shove it in her face. From there we went straight into the first dance and it was really long but amazing. Maranda and I cried and buried our faces into each others necks. we attempted a spin which we failed hilariously at. We nailed the next one. The song was so epic and lovely.  We followed that with the daddy daughter dances, Doug was missing unfortunately and Keith ended up going first. I ran to find Doug and we came back just in time to get him dancing with Maranda. It was very sweet and they both cried hard.  Me and mom went next and we cried so hard as well. I whispered to her that she was an amazing mom and did not need to preface that with undercutting negative comments. It was very sweet.  After the dances dj Pauly attempted the years married ruse to get people dancing. Which was kind of awkward. We eventually got out and went to change. I quickly got into comfier clothes and got back out there dancing. From this point on I started feeling a lot better and a lot drunker. I walked around greeting people. Occasionally me and Maranda would reunite. The dancing was a little awkward. It felt like the music selection was kind of cliche. I requested a bad bunny song.  Weed kept getting offered to me and I kept avoiding it thank god. I did not need that. More power to the people having a great toasty time. Jason Byington said goodbye to us along with a few other people. Things started to thin out from there. Grandma Madge showed up and it was fun to reconnect and thank her for coming out.  The dancing got a little more crazy later into the night. I pulled Devan and Eric into it and surfed back and forth between them, Matt’s group, Selenes group, my family. It was very surreal and lovely to have everyone important to me on a dance floor together. Things got so crazy by the end of the night, even Mom and Dad were going so hard. I saw my dad dancing with his sisters which was very sweet. Matt’s group are such rockstars, I got to dance ballroom style with Dallin and Micheal. I got Sarah out and got her dancing. She seemed to struggle for most of the night. I hope she enjoyed herself even a little. I was in a dancing circle a few time and rocked that shit. (Probably not well) Regi was great and honestly so fun to have there as well. I can’t wait to see her pictures.   We had the last song of the night and went into our sendoff. It was amazing. Dad was pretty concerned that I wasn’t good to drive which I probably wasn’t.. after we got out of the canyon we pulled into a grocery store parking lot and got so excited that we are now married. We talked about everything that happened through the night. It was so fun to hang with her in a parking lot after our wedding. I love her so much. Mom and Dad came to pick us up and they took us to our hotel. We crashed hard.  What an amazing day. I feels some slight regrets about not reaching out to more of the misfits, about not being there for more moments.  But it’s pretty easy to let them slide off my back, I’m incredibly grateful for such an iconic night celebrating me and Maranda.
